notes: Sub 5 (T-scaled mpnet) blended 50/50 with subject_mean_acc as a stabilizer (slide 31, Family 4: metadata as 'stabilizer for sparse rows'). p_final = 0.5 * p_sub5 + 0.5 * subject_mean_acc. Hypothesis: sub 5 has AUC=0.63 (modest ranking signal) and NLL=-0.65 (good but not great calibration). Subject mean is a robust prior with no per-item variance. Equal-weight arithmetic blend should preserve most of sub 5's ranking while reducing per-item variance, improving NLL. If it beats -0.65, the blend hypothesis is right; if it ties, sub 5's prediction is already pulling toward subject_mean (it includes that feature). Reuses the full sub 5 artifact set unchanged; only model.py differs.
